item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Noting that the page has been reverted by me from an edit made by someone without permission since no error was made.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm not crying, that's just my brain sweating from how much I learned

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm not sure how that's a twist. The Foundation holds objects, beings, and locations that are anomalous. That doesn't mean that they're evil.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is both adorable and excellently well-written. I absolutely love the successful experiment giving D-14417 a new shot at life. Thanks for brightening my day with this work.

+1, because I can't give it more than just one point.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
great work. I appreciate this subtle contribution to the canon that D-classes aren't just mass executed at the end of their time with the Foundation. It left me hoping the foundation uses this to rehabilitate more of them. And anything that gets me thinking about the bigger picture, the way this whole thing works, really does it for me.

an enthusiastic +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: [insert blingee'd gif of two cats hugging]

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Finally got around to reading this. My cold dead heart has been sufficiently warmed.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
i couldnt help but smile reading this. it's a very cute concept and i love how there's no ulterior motives or evil-ness or whatever that usually comes with SCP. i really like the emotion and personality shown in Miss J, and the fact that she seems to just be happy keeping kids company when their parents can't watch them n wishes she could help them more.

+1 i would definitely play this and say hello to miss j if she was real
i love you guys. 😭

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The second wholesome digital SCP I've seen lately, the other one being 5270, and this is also the second wholesome digital SCP I've loved recently. +1

item.title: Re: Just Brilliant
item.content:encoded: edit: lol

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Staff notice: this user has been permanently banned for sockpuppeting and ban evasion.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So, let me get this straight. In 15-30 hours, I can get a graduate level education on a particular subject, it's so engaging I could keep going, and once upon a time it was sold for (presumably) a reasonable price that working class parents could afford? I'll fight the foundation for a copy.

That said, I noticed that Miss J doesn't know where she gets her knowledge from but is also was ignorant of herself. It's foundation testing time!
- A history of itself and it's own construction?
- Knowledge of the Foundation and Groups of Interest?
- Knowledge of things that don't exist or make no sense?
- Knowledge of things that might make the SCP uncomfortable, like torture methods?

Of course, that's not the intent of the article. It's a feel good piece and it does that very well. Going with that theme, I want to know more about the other students who used it. Perhaps a kid had no friends, and used it every single day after school. Is he/she a genius leading expert in their field? Did he/she use it to learn social skills and is now very popular?

item.title: Re: +1
item.content:encoded: thank you so much :’]]]

This was an entertaining and joyful read! The high number of upvotes in such a short time is completely deserved and I'll be adding onto that!

The art is fantastic, I love Miss J.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's very cute, I genuinely enjoyed reading this! The character is super cute and the whole article definitely gives off the vibes of random education software you might've had when you were a kid. Though if anything, it seems rather strange that the foundation would let their researcher act so personal with the SCP, though not a gripe, I doubt the entry would have been so charming without it.

+1, Miss J is best girl

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There are a lot of other articles which do this. Presumably they were amnesticized after the fact.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I generally liked this, but it gets a no-vote from me solely because I find the idea that the Foundation would expose the public, children no less, to an anomaly for testing purposes really bothers me. But other than that, I liked this piece.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Huh, I guess it was just a matter of taste.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The stuff about Xenia being taught the necessary skills to work at the foundation was actually in the original draft, but people seemed to think it was contrived, so I ended up cutting it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I’ve changed my vote on this after a reread.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: my headcanon is that they give miss j some basic info that would help her train new employees (because she can’t teach things that the average person would have no way of knowing) and she’s integrated into their training structure, which helps morale a lot :]] i’m glad you liked it!!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This was super wholesome. There's been a lot of stories coming out lately that are about the Foundation abusing and/or killing the shit out of AIs, and it was really starting to depress me.

item.title: Re: A cloud based rehabilitation center for internet runaways and the socially inept.
item.content:encoded:
A very enjoyable and wholesome read. Good descriptions (including experiments etc.)
Also great to have an interview which applies for both personal and professional conversations.

Definitely +1

Edit: It seems I replied in the wrong section my bad

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really wanted to upvote this. But I can't really justify giving this anything but a no-vote for now. The concept is neat and art is fantastic. You really brought Ms. J to life through the characterisation in the logs and the picture at the start of the article. However the story as a whole lacks verisimilitude. I understand that sometimes a writer will have to hand-wave certain aspects of a given narrative to make it work on a conceptual level, but the verisimiliar issue is so blatant to me that it actually took me out of the story.

I can't get past the fact staff are allowed to interview an entity they've already come into contact with prior to their employment. That is a massive conflict of interest! There were plenty of ways you could've avoided this issue with a little format restructuring, such as perhaps Mr. J recognising auxiliary staff setting up the computer she's running on. You could've had them converse in a non-professional setting that would've made sense.

As a writer it can be difficult to weigh up these factors, do I keep a strict verisimilitude throughout the narrative? Do I play a little fast and loose with it for a more expansive narrative? Etc.

Idk, maybe it's a personal taste issue and not so much anything that needs improving with your article per-say. But yeah, that really took me out of the story. I'm honestly really conflicted about my vote on this one and I may even change it to a positive one upon re-reading again later.

To end on a more positive note I think you've crafted a really neat character with a great emotional tone running through the entire piece. It's also refreshing to see a nice wholesome skip for once that's not unbearably OTT.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This site is so full of horrors that an honest to god wholesome SCP was a really welcome change. I really enjoyed reading this, excellent work!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: edited to fix this and some other stuff :'D

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I enjoyed this one a lot. +1.

item.title: Re: No Vote
item.content:encoded: It definitely does! You've earned an upvote from me! Good job! :D and np! =]

item.title: Re: Junior Staff Site Critique Team -- Opinion
item.content:encoded: thanks for the comment!! i ended up removing the last addendum and rearranging some things including two researchers having owned the game, hopefully this helps the piece :'D

item.title: Re: No Vote
item.content:encoded: thanks for the comment!! i ended up removing the last addendum and rearranging some things, hopefully this helps the piece :'D

item.title: Junior Staff Site Critique Team -- Opinion
item.content:encoded:
Hey there! So I really wanted to like this. As I said when I critiqued this, Ms. J has a very fun personality, and the interview is really strong emotionally and content wise. But I stand by my opinion that there shouldn't be both a researcher who had a copy as a kid as the un-neutralization of it, and a researcher who had the game as a kid that ends up interviewing the character. I know you're trying to set up that all these researchers had the game as kids to set up quote

they hired them because a) the game advertised to neglectful parents, which produced b) socially isolated but very intelligent people

but I just think that the ending is incredibly weak. Like, something about that specific message feels off to me. The ending is trying to produce an impactful message, but it really just comes off as overpowered.

Tl;DR: I wanted to like this. I thought the writing was strong, and the interview was very impactful, but is outweighed by the negatives of the (in my opinion) failed attempt at a strong message. I recommend checking out some scps that touch on heavy topics such as SCP-5832 -- Stained, SCP-3183 -- Dissociation, and SCP-4311 -- I care about you..

Good luck in the future!

Please direct any questions to Mew-ltiverse For questions about the Site Criticism Team and what we do, please contact the captain of the Site Criticism Team, SoullessSingularity, or myself.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Overall I’m a fan, but one small irk: Is there a specific reason why Amani needed to be surprised at the beginning of the interview? It stretches my belief that the Foundation ran a test without briefing their researcher fully, and “time constraints” sounds like an ass-pull.

Overall a +1

Edit: Offending ass-pull removed. Woot woot!

item.title: No Vote
item.content:encoded: I reeaaaallllyyyyyyy want to upvote this, but the last addendum just weakens the whole thing for me. I guess it's because it feels unnecessary and just kind of, weird? It doesn't add anything, and doesn't feel like a satisfying closer. Your piece would be much stronger without it. So for now, I'm sticking at a no vote. I really like the rest of this piece, the art and especially the specific graphic you have for it is just so neat. The rest of this piece is awesome. Without the last addendum, this would be a very strong piece.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Amazing work. So happy I got to see this go from a draft to a completed piece.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Just fantastic. If anyone needs an example of how to elicit an emotional response through writing, this would be my go-to. Not a single wasted word.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: i LOVE this thank you so much ;__;!!!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wholesome. I like the whole thing where the twist is that it's actually not evil.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is very cute and I made a fanart CD case booklet cover. I appreciate the little details in the article.

+ Image
– hide block

(edutainment apple source: here)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It kinda reminds me of some of the vibes of my SCP-5045, but taken in the completely opposite direction, and I fucking love that.

1+
Still so sweet and adorable. Good work!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
so happy i got to crit this!!! such an excellent read

+1 :)

item.title: A cloud based rehabilitation center for internet runaways and the socially inept.
item.content:encoded:
Did I hear you right, did I hear you sayin'
That you're gonna make a copy of a game without payin'?
Come on, guys, I thought you knew better
Don't copy that floppy!

Thanks to JusticePrevails, christoffel, DianaBerry, Henzoid, and UraniumEmpire for crit.
Special thanks to stephlynch for their phenomenal work creating the page image, and to daviiid for being my fwiend. :]

check out this wonderful reading by TheeSherm!!!! it's pretty much canon to how miss j sounds in my head :DDD

the super awesome original image made by stephlynch is here :]

super duper!
ty stephlynch :]

Image cred:

+ show block
– hide block

Miss J is my own design. stephlynch made her a 3D model and rigged, animated, and rendered her. I edited her onto the CRT, the sources for which are below. :D
https://www.flickr.com/photos/49503178596@N01/285401
https://www.flickr.com/photos/25185435@N00/3176178164
https://photomosh.com/help/
